tweets.title: India is exploring joint engine development with France's Safran and the UK's Rolls-Royce for the AMCA fighter project. This shift comes after commercial talks with US-based General Electric (GE) hit a major roadblock over surging cost demands (nearly 300% higher than early estimates) and restrictive technology transfer terms.
tweets.content:
India is exploring joint engine development with France's Safran and the UK's Rolls-Royce for the AMCA fighter project. This shift comes after commercial talks with US-based General Electric (GE) hit a major roadblock over surging cost demands (nearly 300% higher than early estimates) and restrictive technology transfer terms.

Netram Defence Review (@NetramDefence)

GE demands 300% cost increase for AMCA engines.

India’s negotiations with GE Aerospace over the F414 fighter jet engine have reportedly stalled after the projected cost surged from ₹70–80 crore per engine to over ₹200 crore during commercial discussions.

The F414 is slated to power the HAL Tejas Mk2 and serve as the interim engine for the Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA Mk-1).

- Engine cost up nearly 300%
- Proposed Indian assembly line could require over $800 million (~₹6,000 crore) in investment
- Negotiations continue over technology transfer, localisation, production, and support packages
- India is also evaluating alternative partnerships with Safran and Rolls-Royce

— https://nitter.net/NetramDefence/status/2069701706888392994#m
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: Mango Diplomacy: BJP Chief Nitin Nabin sends Mangoes🥭 to foreign missions in Delhi
tweets.content:
Mango Diplomacy: BJP Chief Nitin Nabin sends Mangoes🥭 to foreign missions in Delhi

Dr Vijay Chauthaiwale (@vijai63)

Inspired by Prime Minister Narendra Modi Ji’s remarks on the significance of mangoes in Mann Ki Baat, BJP National President Shri Nitin Nabin Ji @NitinNabin sent mangoes to eighty two Head of Missions in New Delhi with a personal note.

With each of four varieties of mangos (Kesar, Dusseheri, Banaganapalli and Langda) carrying their unique flavour, taste and story of its region, this gesture reflects the timeless Indian ethos of Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam—sharing not just a fruit, but India’s culture of sharing joy and sweetness. @narendramodi @BJP4India @overseas_bjp

— https://nitter.net/vijai63/status/2069720330592264595#m

tweets.title: BREAKING: BrahMos-NG Expected by 2028–29 The BrahMos-NG (Next Generation) air-launched supersonic cruise missile is expected to be ready by 2028–2029, according to Alexander Maksichev, speaking to Sputnik. The program is a joint venture between India's DRDO and Russia's NPO Mashinostroyeniya. The smaller size allows for better integration on a wider variety of platforms, including ships, submarines, and aircraft. Fighter jets like the HAL Tejas and Su-30MKI will be able to carry multiple missiles at once.
tweets.content:
BREAKING: BrahMos-NG Expected by 2028–29

The BrahMos-NG (Next Generation) air-launched supersonic cruise missile is expected to be ready by 2028–2029, according to Alexander Maksichev, speaking to Sputnik.

The program is a joint venture between India's DRDO and Russia's NPO Mashinostroyeniya. The smaller size allows for better integration on a wider variety of platforms, including ships, submarines, and aircraft.

Fighter jets like the HAL Tejas and Su-30MKI will be able to carry multiple missiles at once.


Video
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: RT by @NetramDefence: Manipur police & security forces arrested 4 persons in separate anti-narcotics ops conducted in Imphal East & Thoubal, recovering suspected brown sugar, on June 22. On June 23, police arrested 3 persons in Thoubal and recovered 2 soap cases of ~80 g of suspected brown sugar.
tweets.content:
Manipur police & security forces arrested 4 persons in separate anti-narcotics ops conducted in Imphal East & Thoubal, recovering suspected brown sugar, on June 22.

On June 23, police arrested 3 persons in Thoubal and recovered 2 soap cases of ~80 g of suspected brown sugar.

tweets.title: GE demands 300% cost increase for AMCA engines. India’s negotiations with GE Aerospace over the F414 fighter jet engine have reportedly stalled after the projected cost surged from ₹70–80 crore per engine to over ₹200 crore during commercial discussions. The F414 is slated to power the HAL Tejas Mk2 and serve as the interim engine for the Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA Mk-1). - Engine cost up nearly 300% - Proposed Indian assembly line could require over $800 million (~₹6,000 crore) in investment - Negotiations continue over technology transfer, localisation, production, and support packages - India is also evaluating alternative partnerships with Safran and Rolls-Royce
tweets.content:
GE demands 300% cost increase for AMCA engines.

India’s negotiations with GE Aerospace over the F414 fighter jet engine have reportedly stalled after the projected cost surged from ₹70–80 crore per engine to over ₹200 crore during commercial discussions.

The F414 is slated to power the HAL Tejas Mk2 and serve as the interim engine for the Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA Mk-1).

- Engine cost up nearly 300%
- Proposed Indian assembly line could require over $800 million (~₹6,000 crore) in investment
- Negotiations continue over technology transfer, localisation, production, and support packages
- India is also evaluating alternative partnerships with Safran and Rolls-Royce

tweets.title: IRAN: MISSILE PROGRAMME ‘NOT NEGOTIABLE’ Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian has stated that Iran's missile programme was not included in the 14-point memorandum of understanding (MoU) with the United States and will not be part of any future agreement. Pezeshkian said: “Without missiles, we'd be like Gaza.” He emphasized that Iran's ballistic missile capabilities are a core national security asset and firmly rejected any linkage between missile restrictions and ongoing diplomatic negotiations with Washington.
tweets.content:
IRAN: MISSILE PROGRAMME ‘NOT NEGOTIABLE’

Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian has stated that Iran's missile programme was not included in the 14-point memorandum of understanding (MoU) with the United States and will not be part of any future agreement. Pezeshkian said:

“Without missiles, we'd be like Gaza.”

He emphasized that Iran's ballistic missile capabilities are a core national security asset and firmly rejected any linkage between missile restrictions and ongoing diplomatic negotiations with Washington.

tweets.title: Bangladesh is reportedly moving ahead with plans to acquire 24 Chinese J-10CE multirole fighter jets as part of a broader expansion of defence ties with Beijing. The deal, reportedly worth around $960 million, could be finalized by August and comes amid growing China-Bangladesh cooperation in defence, infrastructure, trade, and investment. The J-10CE is China's export variant of the J-10C and is equipped with advanced radar, long-range air-to-air missiles, and multirole strike capabilities.
tweets.content:
Bangladesh is reportedly moving ahead with plans to acquire 24 Chinese J-10CE multirole fighter jets as part of a broader expansion of defence ties with Beijing.

The deal, reportedly worth around $960 million, could be finalized by August and comes amid growing China-Bangladesh cooperation in defence, infrastructure, trade, and investment.

The J-10CE is China's export variant of the J-10C and is equipped with advanced radar, long-range air-to-air missiles, and multirole strike capabilities.

END OF THE BRIEF
tweets.content:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
23rd Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● The Iranian ambassador to the UN in Geneva warned that the red line in negotiations with Washington is that Israel must cease attacks on Lebanon.

● The US president claimed that Iran has completely agreed to nuclear inspections long into the future, though Iranian authorities stated that the UN's nuclear watchdog will not be able to inspect key nuclear sites bombed by the US and Israel last year.

● Israel and Lebanon began a fifth round of talks in Washington, with Lebanese officials insisting that face-to-face negotiations with Israel are the only way to secure an end to the conflict.

● The Israeli military reported that its forces opened fire at Hezbollah fighters in southern Lebanon who approached soldiers while posing as civilians.

● Technical talks between Iran and the United States in Switzerland have concluded, with both sides agreeing to establish negotiating groups focused on nuclear issues and sanctions.

●Iran's chief negotiator stated that Tehran will administer the Strait of Hormuz following talks aimed at ending the war.

●Iran's judiciary announced that over 3,000 individuals have been arrested in recent months, including hundreds accused of carrying out operational actions for Israel.

END OF THE BRIEF

Netram Defence Review (@NetramDefence)

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
22nd Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● The IDF eliminated 3 armed terrorists from Hamas’ military wing who were attempting to carry out attacks against IDF troops.

● Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Baqaei stated that a threatening US statement during the four-party session prompted Iran to declare it would not continue the talks for now.

● The US Treasury Department issued a 60-day general license, effective until 21 August 2026, authorizing the production, delivery, and sale of Iranian-origin petroleum.

● Iran’s Foreign Ministry spokesman announced that Tehran’s interaction with the IAEA will be subject to approval by Iran’s Parliament and the Supreme National Security Council.

● The French and Qatari-owned gas carriers WADI AL SAIL and MEKAINES were allowed to transit the Strait of Hormuz this morning, both en route to Ras Laffan LNG terminal.

END OF THE BRIEF

tweets.title: 🚨 Official IAF Records Sink Pakistan's Rafale Claims 📍 Pakistan's claims of multiple Rafale losses during Operation Sindoor have been contradicted by official Indian Air Force records. 📍 An Air Headquarters Request for Proposal (RFP), accessed through a "Reply to Request for Information under the RTI Act, 2005", confirms that all 36 Rafale fighter jets inducted by the IAF remain operational. 📍 The fleet strength was recorded at 36 aircraft as of June 18, 2026. 📍 The disclosure effectively dismantles months of claims and propaganda surrounding alleged Rafale losses. 📍 India inducted all 36 Rafales between 2020 and 2022. 📍 The aircraft are currently operated by No. 17 Squadron "Golden Arrows" at Ambala and No. 101 Squadron "Falcons" at Hasimara.
tweets.content:
🚨 Official IAF Records Sink Pakistan's Rafale Claims

📍 Pakistan's claims of multiple Rafale losses during Operation Sindoor have been contradicted by official Indian Air Force records.

📍 An Air Headquarters Request for Proposal (RFP), accessed through a "Reply to Request for Information under the RTI Act, 2005", confirms that all 36 Rafale fighter jets inducted by the IAF remain operational.

📍 The fleet strength was recorded at 36 aircraft as of June 18, 2026.

📍 The disclosure effectively dismantles months of claims and propaganda surrounding alleged Rafale losses.

📍 India inducted all 36 Rafales between 2020 and 2022.

📍 The aircraft are currently operated by No. 17 Squadron "Golden Arrows" at Ambala and No. 101 Squadron "Falcons" at Hasimara.
tweets.author: @OsintTV

tweets.title: The only reason I look the other way when DefX talks about schemes that provide financial assistance to the country’s marginalised communities is because that is a very small cost to pay to keep the right people in the right offices. Always notice one thing: Pakistanis, Bangladeshis, and other adversaries of the Indian state rarely attack the Indian Armed Forces or the intelligence community directly. They attack the political structure because they know where the backing that enables these institutions to do what they believe is best for the country comes from. We could become extremely vulnerable within a couple of years if we do not have strong and stable leadership at the Centre for at least the next decade. There are too many wolves around us, lads.
tweets.content:
The only reason I look the other way when DefX talks about schemes that provide financial assistance to the country’s marginalised communities is because that is a very small cost to pay to keep the right people in the right offices.

Always notice one thing: Pakistanis, Bangladeshis, and other adversaries of the Indian state rarely attack the Indian Armed Forces or the intelligence community directly. They attack the political structure because they know where the backing that enables these institutions to do what they believe is best for the country comes from.

We could become extremely vulnerable within a couple of years if we do not have strong and stable leadership at the Centre for at least the next decade.

There are too many wolves around us, lads.

tweets.content:
The standoff with Nihangs at Nagrasu Gurudwara looks very interesting. I checked the location of the Gurudwara.

Found the location of it pretty interesting.

I am pleased to see the administration and security forces acting mindfully; all the enemy wants is blood on a religious site, especially of Sikhs. They have been trying for it but have failed so far. This standoff provides them with a unique opportunity.

I hope whoever commands these Nihangs steps in for a peaceful cool-down.

tweets.title: BREAKING: Jaipur Woman Arrested Over Jaish-e-Mohammed Links The Rajasthan ATS has arrested a woman from Jaipur for maintaining contact with members of the Pakistan-based terror outfit Jaish-e-Mohammed and other anti-national elements. She has been booked under the UAPA, with investigators examining her digital communications, social media activity, and foreign contacts. According to reports, the suspect was preparing for travel to Pakistan and had undergone religious conversion. Authorities are also probing online accounts, deleted data, and possible links to overseas operatives.
tweets.content:
BREAKING: Jaipur Woman Arrested Over Jaish-e-Mohammed Links

The Rajasthan ATS has arrested a woman from Jaipur for maintaining contact with members of the Pakistan-based terror outfit Jaish-e-Mohammed and other anti-national elements.

She has been booked under the UAPA, with investigators examining her digital communications, social media activity, and foreign contacts.

According to reports, the suspect was preparing for travel to Pakistan and had undergone religious conversion. Authorities are also probing online accounts, deleted data, and possible links to overseas operatives.

tweets.title: India and the UAE in early-stage talks to export the BrahMos after its steller performance in Operation Sindoor. India is in early-stage talks with the UAE to export the BrahMos supersonic cruise missile. The countries are also discussing the sale of Akashteer, an automated air defense system developed by India which was extensively deployed against Pakistan during Operation Sindoor. This follows a massive jump in India's defense exports, which crossed $4 billion in early 2026.
tweets.content:
India and the UAE in early-stage talks to export the BrahMos after its steller performance in Operation Sindoor.

India is in early-stage talks with the UAE to export the BrahMos supersonic cruise missile. The countries are also discussing the sale of Akashteer, an automated air defense system developed by India which was extensively deployed against Pakistan during Operation Sindoor. This follows a massive jump in India's defense exports, which crossed $4 billion in early 2026.

tweets.title: Interesting detail from the IAF’s June 2026 Rafale sustainment RFP. While ISPR claimed that 3 Rafales were shot down, Air Headquarters is seeking bridge support for a fleet of 36 Rafale aircraft, the same number India originally procured. To make it more interesting, we’ve already seen at least two Rafale tail numbers that Pakistani accounts had claimed were “shot down” flying afterward.
tweets.content:
Interesting detail from the IAF’s June 2026 Rafale sustainment RFP.

While ISPR claimed that 3 Rafales were shot down, Air Headquarters is seeking bridge support for a fleet of 36 Rafale aircraft, the same number India originally procured.

To make it more interesting, we’ve already seen at least two Rafale tail numbers that Pakistani accounts had claimed were “shot down” flying afterward.

tweets.title: ‘Won't return to pre-war conditions’: Iran says Hormuz will be administered by them Iran's chief negotiator and parliamentary speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf on Tuesday said the Strait of Hormuz would remain under Tehran's administration and would not return to its pre-war status. Ghalibaf stated that "the Strait of Hormuz will never return to its pre-war conditions and will be administered by the Islamic Republic of Iran,” AFP reported.
tweets.content:
‘Won't return to pre-war conditions’: Iran says Hormuz will be administered by them

Iran's chief negotiator and parliamentary speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf on Tuesday said the Strait of Hormuz would remain under Tehran's administration and would not return to its pre-war status.

Ghalibaf stated that "the Strait of Hormuz will never return to its pre-war conditions and will be administered by the Islamic Republic of Iran,” AFP reported.
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title: For more than a week, I’ve been sitting on this because the same people have spent years branding anyone who asks questions for a better IAF as “anti-Rafale” and, at times, even “anti-Air Force.” Yes, the Rafale engine PBL has expired. A bridge arrangement is being signed while a larger contract is negotiated and the entire PBL framework is renegotiated. But now that this document is all over the place, there’s no point pretending otherwise.
tweets.content:
For more than a week, I’ve been sitting on this because the same people have spent years branding anyone who asks questions for a better IAF as “anti-Rafale” and, at times, even “anti-Air Force.”

Yes, the Rafale engine PBL has expired. A bridge arrangement is being signed while a larger contract is negotiated and the entire PBL framework is renegotiated.

But now that this document is all over the place, there’s no point pretending otherwise.
tweets.author: @alpha_defense

tweets.title: A Navy veteran once explained the difference between an SSN and an SSBN to a group of fighter-jet enthusiasts: SSN = Fighter jet SSBN = Bomber An SSN is fast, agile, relatively smaller, and most importantly extremely quiet. An SSBN is a nightmare in hibernation. And once awakened, the target country may end up in hibernation instead. That’s why the journey from SSBN to SSN is a significant technological leap. Going the other way around would have been much easier. In the process, you would also likely develop highly capable SSKs along the way.
tweets.content:
A Navy veteran once explained the difference between an SSN and an SSBN to a group of fighter-jet enthusiasts:

SSN = Fighter jet
SSBN = Bomber

An SSN is fast, agile, relatively smaller, and most importantly extremely quiet.

An SSBN is a nightmare in hibernation. And once awakened, the target country may end up in hibernation instead.

That’s why the journey from SSBN to SSN is a significant technological leap. Going the other way around would have been much easier. In the process, you would also likely develop highly capable SSKs along the way.

Somnath Mukherjee (@somnath1978)

How much of a "stretch" is an SSN frm an SSBN - instead of mucking arnd w/212 et al, why not crank out 6 SSN?

— https://nitter.net/somnath1978/status/2069054209245552760#m
